title: ranunculus bonariensis subsp. bonariensis
intro: Ranunculus bonariensis subsp. bonariensis, also known as the small-flowered buttercup, is a plant species in the buttercup family, Ranunculaceae, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus bonariensis subsp. bonariensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing, making it a potential treatment for skin conditions and minor injuries. Ranunculus bonariensis subsp. bonariensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its roots and leaves can be used in folk remedies, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
